findings.title: Provider auto-selection decision not logged
findings.summary: When auto provider resolution selects a non-local-subprocess provider, the resolved name is not logged. Only the fallback to local-subprocess emits a warning; every other resolution is silent, making it hard to trace which provider was chosen in production logs.
evidence: packages/remediate-code/src/providers/index.ts:120-126 - only the local-subprocess fallback path emits a process.stderr.write warning; all other auto-resolved paths (claude-code, opencode, subprocess-template, vscode-task) produce no log output

findings.title: Subprocess spawn errors logged only to reject callback, no stderr trace
findings.summary: In spawnLoggedCommand the error event on the child process calls session.fail() which rejects the promise, but does not write any diagnostic line to stderr before doing so. The runId and command are not surfaced in a log entry at failure time.
evidence: packages/remediate-code/src/providers/spawnLoggedCommand.ts:230 - spawnedChild.on('error', ...) calls session.fail(err, reject) with no structured log entry written to stderr before rejecting

findings.title: Quota scheduler wave size reductions carry no log trace
findings.summary: scheduleWave in scheduler.ts computes a wave_size that may be significantly smaller than requestedConcurrency due to cooldown, TPM cap, or host concurrency limits. No log line is emitted when the returned wave_size differs from the input, leaving throttling decisions invisible unless the caller chooses to surface them.
evidence: packages/remediate-code/src/quota/scheduler.ts:88-95 - when cooldown_until is active and waveSize is forced to 1, no log statement is emitted
packages/remediate-code/src/quota/scheduler.ts:148-162 - the final wave_size is returned with no logging of adjustments applied

findings.title: scheduleWave silently swallows quota state read errors
findings.summary: In waveScheduler.ts the readQuotaState call inside scheduleWave is wrapped in a bare try/catch that discards the error entirely with no log output. When quota state is unreadable, the scheduler falls back to no learned state, but the operator has no way to know this degradation occurred.
evidence: packages/remediate-code/src/steps/waveScheduler.ts:97-101 - catch block contains only the comment 'Best-effort: proceed without learned state' and no log write, discarding any I/O error or parse failure silently

findings.title: Missing worker result warnings lack run_id context
findings.summary: In dispatch.ts mergeDocumentResults and mergeImplementResults, missing worker result files are reported via console.warn with the result path but without the run_id. This makes it hard to correlate the warning with a specific run in aggregated logs.
evidence: packages/remediate-code/src/steps/dispatch.ts:423 - console.warn omits run_id and phase when reporting missing document worker result
packages/remediate-code/src/steps/dispatch.ts:625 - console.warn similarly omits structured context for missing implement worker result

findings.title: unhandled_state reached with no structured stderr emission
findings.summary: In nextStep.ts the unhandled_state terminal path writes a user-facing prompt but emits no structured machine-readable entry to stderr with state.status and runId. Operators monitoring stderr receive no signal about an unexpected state transition.
evidence: packages/remediate-code/src/steps/nextStep.ts:1531-1554 - the unhandled_state branch returns writeCurrentStep result but writes nothing to process.stderr before or after doing so

findings.title: hashFile and hashFileSync swallow all errors silently
findings.summary: In fileIntegrity.ts both hashFile and hashFileSync return undefined when any error occurs during hashing, with no log output. A permissions error or unexpected I/O failure during the pre-implementation integrity check is invisible to operators.
evidence: packages/remediate-code/src/utils/fileIntegrity.ts:9-14 - hashFileSync catch block returns undefined with no logging
packages/remediate-code/src/utils/fileIntegrity.ts:17-24 - hashFile async catch block also returns undefined silently

findings.title: CompositeQuotaSource silently swallows source query failures
findings.summary: In compositeQuotaSource.ts each source query is wrapped in a try/catch that discards errors with no logging. When a quota source fails, the composite source silently falls through to the next source with no record of the failure.
evidence: packages/shared/src/quota/compositeQuotaSource.ts:14-19 - catch block is entirely empty, discarding errors from quota sources

findings.title: Quota state fallback logs use plain text without structured fields
findings.summary: In state.ts the quota state read fallback paths emit plain text to process.stderr without structured key=value fields or a consistent log level indicator that telemetry consumers could parse.
evidence: packages/shared/src/quota/state.ts:66-68 - plain text '[quota] ignoring invalid quota state at ...' with no structured fields
packages/shared/src/quota/state.ts:73-77 - similar plain text log for unreadable quota state, no structured context

findings.title: Global mutable singletons for escalation and dedup state hinder testability
findings.summary: Both autoescalate.ts and dedup.ts use module-level mutable singletons as their entire state store. Tests must call reset functions between runs, and any parallel uses share state, making the code fragile to change.
evidence: packages/audit-code/.tmp/opentoken/.opencode/plugins/opentoken/autoescalate.ts:22-28 - module-level const state holds all escalation tracking; exported reset function required to restore clean state
packages/audit-code/.tmp/opentoken/.opencode/plugins/opentoken/dedup.ts:15-17 - module-level recentCalls array and callCounter hold cross-call dedup state

findings.title: applyUltraCompression contains dead-code line-protection loop
findings.summary: In autoescalate.ts the loop that nominally protects code lines in applyUltraCompression maps each line through a conditional but both branches return the original line unchanged, making the map a no-op that adds complexity without effect.
evidence: packages/audit-code/.tmp/opentoken/.opencode/plugins/opentoken/autoescalate.ts:174-179 - the map callback returns line in both the isCodeLine branch and the else branch; the entire protectedLines transform is a no-op

findings.title: filterCargoBuild duplicates block-flush logic across multiple branches
findings.summary: In cargo.ts filterCargoBuild the block accumulation and flush logic is repeated in three separate branches with slightly inconsistent handling, making future changes error-prone.
evidence: packages/audit-code/.tmp/opentoken/.opencode/plugins/opentoken/families/cargo.ts:19-42 - the block push/flush pattern repeats across the error[, warning[, and inBlock branches with different array targets and differing restart logic
